global_rules: No small buttons or interactive elements; this is a passive 16:9 TV display
Data must be completely mocked (demo/fallback data) as requested by user
Auto-rotate between 4 distinct screens every 15 seconds
Auto-refresh data (if it were real) every 60 seconds
German language (Deutsch) for all static text

layout.structure: 16:9 fixed fullscreen (h-screen w-screen overflow-hidden). Do not allow scrolling.
layout.spacing: Generous overscan margins: p-12 or p-16 for the main container.
layout.cards: Use 'Grid Borders / Technical Look'. 1px borders (border-blue-400/20), flat background (bg-[#111A3A]/80), sharp corners (rounded-none or max rounded-md).

screens.layout: Grid list of 4-6 matches. Each row showing Time, Team A Flag/Name, 'vs', Team B Flag/Name.
Hero layout. Centered massive vs matchup. 'ANPFIFF IN XX MINUTEN' (Kickoff in XX mins).
Bento grid of currently active matches. Massive score typography. Pulsing red dot with 'LIVE' text and current minute (e.g., '67. Min').
Grid list of completed matches. Text slightly dimmed (opacity-70) except for the winner which is highlighted.

motion.screen_transition: Framer Motion AnimatePresence. Slide in from right, exit left. Duration 0.8s, ease: 'anticipate'.
motion.micro_interactions: None. This is a non-interactive display.

background_image.usage: Global fixed background with bg-black/40 overlay to ensure text contrast.

jandia_logo.usage: Header top left. Displayed prominently on all screens.

instructions_to_main_agent: Use react-country-flag for team flags if possible, or use emoji fallbacks for countries.
Mock all data in a dedicated service file. Ensure data structure supports live minute updating and score changes for realism.
Implement a rotating carousel wrapper using Framer Motion that cycles the 4 screens every 15 seconds.
Header must include a clock (current time) and a subtle 60s progress bar or indicator showing when the 'data' refreshes.
Ensure the UI uses the 16:9 ratio appropriately, stretching content to fit the screen size nicely using flex/grid.
Use standard HTML/Tailwind for layouts, bypassing Shadcn components like Cards since they are too rigid for large TV displays.
Add 'data-testid' to all key text elements (e.g., 'data-testid="live-score-team1"').
